yieldRouter.poolsNote: 3 honest ERC-4626 vaults (MockERC4626) wrapping REAL Circle/CCTP USDC (0x036CbD…). They do 1:1 custody of the real bridged USDC — no fabricated yield. They are NOT external lending markets: the SP0 gate (2026-07-09, relayer/scripts/check-aave-usdc.mjs) proved no real yield protocol on Base Sepolia accepts CCTP USDC (Aave lists a faucet token; Morpho/Moonwell/Compound are Base-mainnet only). Real external yield is demonstrated on the Stellar leg via Blend. Pool1 from SP1; pool2 (0x5E84…) + pool3 (0xadD3…) added 2026-07-06 for the SP3 3-pool catalog.

baseExitSweeper.note: Stateless full-exit sweeper. No owner, no admin surface, no rescue path by design: one instance serves every user, so an owner-gated sweep could take funds in flight for another user's transaction. Reads balances at execution time because a userOp's calldata is fixed at signing and CCTP v2 has no max sentinel.

mainnetAdapter.note: AaveV3Adapter4626 is the MAINNET-READY replacement for the testnet demo vaults (unit-tested + proven against real Aave via Base-mainnet fork test — base-contracts/test/AaveV3Adapter.fork.t.sol). NOT deployed on testnet (Aave Base Sepolia lists faucet USDC, not Circle USDC). At the mainnet flip: deploy 3× AaveV3Adapter4626 against the targets below and swap YieldRouter.allowedPools to the adapter addresses (script pattern: base-contracts/script/DeployAdapters.s.sol).
